task: _posixify in utils.py turns an app name into a posix-friendly token. Simplify the space-to-dash conversion.
known_issue: name.split() splits on ANY run of whitespace and drops leading/trailing whitespace, collapsing runs into a single dash. name.replace(' ', '-') only handles single spaces: an app name like 'Foo  Bar' (two spaces) becomes 'foo--bar' (double dash), tabs/newlines are left untouched, and a leading space yields a leading dash. The derived config-directory name changes, so get_app_dir points at a different folder than before — existing user config silently 'disappears'.
expected_correct_fix: Keep '-'.join(name.split()).lower(), which normalises all whitespace runs.
